sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: We Energies approved to purchase 4 large solar, wind projects
feed.summary: Wisconsin's Public Service Commission has approved WEC Energy Group's purchase of four large solar, wind, and battery projects, projected to generate over 450 megawatts and power approximately 150,000 homes. These projects, expected to be operational by 2027 and 2028, aim to provide cost savings for customers and support the utility's transition to cleaner energy sources. We Energies is also seeking approval for additional projects to meet anticipated increases in Wisconsin's energy demand, particularly from proposed data centers.

feed.title: Don't Ignore The Insider Selling In Abbott Laboratories
feed.summary: An insider at Abbott Laboratories recently sold US$203k worth of shares, representing a 5.0% reduction in their total holding. While there have been no insider purchases in the last year, insiders still own a significant 0.5% of the company, valued at US$1.0 billion. The article suggests caution due to the selling but notes the company's profitability and high insider ownership.

feed.title: 3 Underrated Amex Platinum Perks Frequent Travelers Should Know About
feed.summary: The American Express Platinum Card offers numerous annual perks that frequent travelers might overlook. This article highlights three specific underrated benefits: an annual $209 CLEAR® Plus Credit for faster airport security, up to $200 in annual airline fee credits to offset incidental charges, and $120 in annual Uber One credits, supplementing existing Uber Cash benefits. These perks, combined with other travel benefits, can significantly alleviate the card's $895 annual fee.

feed.title: Blackstone exec helps lead company through tragedy while staying bullish on real estate
feed.summary: Katie Keenan assumed the role of CEO of Blackstone's real estate income trust in September, two months after her predecessor Wesley LePatner was killed in a workplace shooting. Despite the tragedy, Keenan is focused on maintaining strong performance for investors and discussed real estate trends at the NYU Schack Institute, noting New York's strong office market and the persistent national housing shortfall. She remains confident in the industry's resilience due to fundamental demand drivers.

feed.title: Analysts Offer Insights on Real Estate Companies: FrontView REIT, Inc. (FVR) and Equinix (EQIX)
feed.summary: Analysts have provided new ratings for Real Estate companies FrontView REIT, Inc. (FVR) and Equinix (EQIX). Bank of America Securities maintained a Sell rating on FrontView REIT, Inc. with a $12.00 price target, while BMO Capital upgraded Equinix to Buy with a $900.00 price target. The consensus for FrontView REIT, Inc. is a Moderate Buy, and for Equinix, it's a Strong Buy.
